title: Regolamenti dwar Ftehim Bilaterali fuq is-Sigurtà Soċjali bejn Malta u New Zealand
title_en: Regolamenti dwar Ftehim Bilaterali fuq is-Sigurtà Soċjali bejn Malta u New Zealand
description: Official text from legislation.mt (eli/sl/318.23/mlt, expression eli/sl/318.23/20131208/mlt).

provisions.title: L-AVVIŻ LEGALI 435 tal-2013
provisions.content:
It-titolu ta’ dawn ir-regolamenti hu r-Regolamenti dwar
Ftehim Bilaterali fuq is-Sigurtà Soċjali bejn Malta u New Zealand.

provisions.title: Titolu
provisions.content:
F’dawn ir-regolamenti "Ftehim" tfisser il-Ftehim fuq isSigurtà Soċjali bejn il-Gvern ta’ Malta u l-Gvern ta’ New Zealand
iffirmat fit-8 ta’ Lulju, 2013, li kopja tiegħu, flimkien masSupplementary Administrative Arrangement for the
Implementation of the Agreement u l-Administrative Arrangement
for the Apploication of the Agreement, fil-verżjoni originali
tagħhom bl-Ingliż, huma annessi fl-Iskedi li jinsabu ma’ dawn irregolamenti.

provisions.title: Tifsir
provisions.content:
Id-dispożizzjonijiet tal-Att dwar is-Sigurtà Soċjali għandu
jkollhom seħħ fl-applikazzjoni tal-imsemmi Att fir-rigward talpersuni msemmija fil-Ftehim, bla ħsara għal dawk il-modifiki u
adattamenti li jistgħu jkunu meħtieġa skont id-dispożizzjonijiet ta’
dak il-Ftehim, u sa fejn l-Att dwar is-Sigurtà Soċjali japplika għal -

Applikabilità.
Kap. 318.

Pensjonijiet għall-Irtirar; u
Pensjonijiet għar-Romol.

*ara r-regolament 1(2) ta’ dawn ir-Regolamenti, kif promulgati oriġinarjament.

[ L.S.318.23

FTEHIM BILATERALI FUQ IS-SIGURTÀ SOĊJALI
(Artikolu 2)

The Government of New Zealand
And
The Government of Malta
Hereinafter referred to as "the Parties"
RESOLVING to co-operate in the field of Social Security and wishing to
coordinate the operation of their respective social security systems and to enhance
the equitable access by people covered by this Agreement to specified social security
benefits provided under the laws of both countries
HAVE agreed as follows:

provisions.title: eligibility or receipt of particular benefits)
provisions.content:
This Agreement also applies to any domestic law, order, or regulation that
extends the legislation of a Party to new benefits or new categories of beneficiaries
unless a reservation in writing by the Party that is extending the legislation is
communicated to the other Party within 3 months from the date on which the law,
order, or regulation enters into force.

provisions.title: order, or regulation enters into force
provisions.content:
The Parties shall advise each other promptly, of the enactment of laws,
orders or regulations of the kind referred to in paragraph 3 and 4 of this Article.
Article 3
Persons to whom this Agreement applies
This Agreement applies to any person who:
(a) has completed a period of Maltese contributions; or
(b) has completed a period of New Zealand working age residence; or
(c) is a dependent or survivor of a person referred to in paragraph (a) or
paragraph (b) of this Article on whom the legislation of Malta confers
eligibility to Maltese benefits.

FTEHIM BILATERALI FUQ IS-SIGURTÀ SOĊJALI

[ L.S.318.23

Article 4
Equality of Treatment
All persons to whom this Agreement applies shall be treated equally by a Party in
regard to the rights and obligations that arise under the legislation of that Party or as
a result of this Agreement.

provisions.title: transmit it without delay to the appropriate institution of the other Party
provisions.content:
In this Article, appropriate institution includes a court, tribunal, or authority
with jurisdiction to consider appeals or reviews in relation to benefits under the
legislation of a Party, a benefits review committee (in relation to New Zealand), and
a competent institution.

provisions.title: a competent institution
provisions.content:
In relation to Malta, the reference in paragraph 3 of this Article to an appeal
is a reference to a document concerning an appeal that may be made to the Umpire
according to the Social Security Act (Cap 318) as amended from time to time.

provisions.title: according to the Social Security Act (Cap 318) as amended from time to time
provisions.content:
The date on which an application, notice or appeal referred to in paragraphs
3 to 7 of this Article is lodged with the competent institution of a Party shall be
treated, for all purposes concerning the matter to which it relates, as the date of
lodgement of that document with the competent institution of the other Party.
Article 17
Determination of Claims

provisions.title: terms of the provisions of Article 108 of the Social Security Act (Cap 318)
provisions.content:
Destruction of Information Matching Data

In accordance with New Zealand Privacy Act 1992 liaison forms from Malta will
be destroyed within 60 working days from the date of their receipt in New Zealand,
unless a discrepancy is found and a decision is made to take adverse action about
that. In that case, the forms will be destroyed either after 1 year if no adverse action
is subsequently taken or, if adverse action is taken, as soon as practicable after the
forms are no longer needed for the purpose of taking action against an identifiable
individual.
In accordance with the Malta Data Protection Act 2001, liaison forms from New
Zealand will not be kept for a period longer than is necessary. In this regard, forms
from New Zealand will be destroyed within 60 working days from the date of their
receipt in Malta, unless:
(a) a discrepancy is found; or
(b) a decision is made to take action in regard to the discrepancy; or
(c) further investigations and clarifications are required on the matter.
In such cases, depending on the action to be taken, the forms will be destroyed as
soon as practicable after all issues are resolved and the forms are no longer needed to
deliver the service as appropriate to an identifiable individual.
Section 2
Benefit reference numbers
Neither Liaison Agency will re-use or re-assign their benefit reference numbers to
another person.
Section 3
Commencement
This Supplementary Administrative Arrangement will commence on the same day
the Agreement and Administrative Arrangement enters into force and will operate
for the duration of the Agreement.

FTEHIM BILATERALI FUQ IS-SIGURTÀ SOĊJALI

[ L.S.318.23

SIGNED at Valletta this 08th day of July 2013
FOR
THE

FOR
THE

(Artikolu 2)
In accordance with Article 13 of the Agreement on Social Security between the
Government of Malta and the Government of New Zealand, signed on [insert date],
the Competent Authorities hereby make the following Administrative Arrangement.
The Administrative Arrangement is set out in Sections.
Section 1
Definitions

provisions.title: application. Applicants will also be informed of their review and appeal rights
provisions.content:
Provided that the Liaison Agencies have obtained any necessary consent
from their respective Privacy Authorities, the Liaison Agencies may jointly decide in
writing to add, amend or delete items of information to be exchanged.

[ L.S.318.23

FTEHIM BILATERALI FUQ IS-SIGURTÀ SOĊJALI
Section 8
Exchange Rates

The exchange rates to be used are the exchange rates normally used by each
Liaison Agency.
Section 9
Statistical Information
The Liaison Agencies will exchange statistics on an annual basis regarding the
payments which each has made under the Agreement; these statistics will include
data on the number of beneficiaries and the cash value of benefits paid, by type of
benefits.
Section 10
Mutual Assistance and Processing Standards
